verdict.answer_first: B leads with the command in sentence 1: "The simplest way is `jq`:" then `jq . file.json`; A also starts well with "`jq` is the standard tool" but adds install caveat first.
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive: "`jq` is the standard tool" and keeps alternatives secondary; B spreads attention across "Common alternatives," `sponge`, minify, tabs, install paths.
verdict.fabrication: A has an unverified broad claim: "pre-installed on most systems"; B has an overclaim: "python3 -m json.tool is always available."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on: primary command, in-place command, fallback, and 2 useful variants; B adds more commands than needed for a simple CLI pretty-print task.
